formal_model_status.gap: The formal model names write-buffer commands in the command table and generic special-message paths, but does not define a complete executable write-buffer primitive or its post-append clearing boundary.

legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.summary: RAA dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through write-buf; write-buf appends 0 or 1 only when buffer-full? is false.

legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.input_clearing: stem-process-special-message wrapper clears input through zero-buf/zero-hig flow rather than a matching RAA-style input-process boundary
legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.summary: SEMSIM defines write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as appends, but stem-process-special-message wraps the selected operation in zero-hig and zero-buf, erasing the buffer after the append.

legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.input_clearing: write-buf-zero and write-buf-one clear smb and input channels
legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.summary: FSMSIM appends 0 or 1 to the buffer and clears self-mailbox plus input channels, without a matching buffer-full guard.

command_bit_source.formal_model_locus: lines 437-454, command table names write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as distinct commands
command_bit_source.raa_locus: lines 264-273, process-special-message dispatches write-buf-zero to write-buf 0 and write-buf-one to write-buf 1
command_bit_source.semsim_locus: lines 346-365, write-buf-zero appends 0 and write-buf-one appends 1 before the stem wrapper clears buffer/high state
command_bit_source.fsmsim_locus: lines 262-267, write-buf-zero appends 0 and write-buf-one appends 1 while clearing self-mailbox and input channels
command_bit_source.summary: The named write-buffer commands carry literal 0 and 1 append bits. The bit value is not derived from the ordinary standard-signal high-rail comparison path, though high-rail and post-append state clearing remain unresolved.

buffer_full_boundary_resolution.formal_model_locus: lines 152-175, less-than-full write-to-buffer guard and full-buffer process state
buffer_full_boundary_resolution.raa_locus: lines 204-205 and lines 280-282, buffer-full? guard and write-buf append only when not full
buffer_full_boundary_resolution.legacy_absence: SEMSIM and FSMSIM define append functions without a matching named command-token full-buffer rule.
buffer_full_boundary_resolution.summary: AS preserves the existing full-buffer boundary before write-buffer append. The formal model and RAA both gate writes on a less-than-full buffer; SEMSIM and FSMSIM omit a matching guard but provide no contrary full-buffer policy.

post_append_clearing_resolution.raa_locus: lines 231-254 and 264-282, process-special-message clears channels after write-buf appends
post_append_clearing_resolution.fsmsim_locus: lines 240-245 and lines 262-267, command source selection and write-buf-zero/write-buf-one append while clearing smb and input channels
post_append_clearing_resolution.semsim_locus: lines 353-365, stem-process-special-message wraps write-buf in zero-hig and zero-buf
post_append_clearing_resolution.formal_model_locus: lines 170-171 and 674-677, write-to-buffer clears input while preserving buffer/control and special-message handling clears self-mailbox source
post_append_clearing_resolution.legacy_divergence: SEMSIM clears the appended buffer through its stem special-message wrapper; AS preserves the appended buffer because RAA and FSMSIM preserve it while clearing command source/input state.
post_append_clearing_resolution.summary: Write-buffer command execution should preserve the appended literal bit and clear the active command source. High/control state is preserved for the implementation slice unless a later source-backed ADR changes it.

recipient_surface_resolution.summary: Delivered recipient write-buffer command messages stay in the existing recipient non-init rejection boundary. Direct self-mailbox and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer tokens now execute under ADR-0161.

self_target_surface_resolution.summary: AS executes direct self-mailbox write-buffer commands and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer commands by appending the command's literal bit and clearing the active command source. ADR-0162 records both implemented self-target surfaces as integrated evidence bundles.

execution_readiness.summary: Write-buffer append execution is source-resolved and implemented for direct self-mailbox and completed self-target command-buffer surfaces.

resolved_resolution_questions.legacy_divergence: The formal model, RAA, SEMSIM, and FSMSIM agree that write-buf-zero and write-buf-one carry literal 0 and 1 append bits; ADR-0161 implements the source-resolved self-target append surfaces.

AS already rejects delivered recipient write-buffer command messages under UC-RECIPIENT-NON-INIT-COMMAND-MESSAGE-REJECTED; direct self-mailbox and self-target command-buffer write-buffer command tokens execute under ADR-0161.

AS now executes direct self-mailbox write-buffer command tokens under UC-STEM-SELF-MAILBOX-WRITE-BUFFER-APPENDED and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer commands under UC-STEM-COMMAND-BUFFER-SELF-WRITE-BUFFER-APPENDED; standard-signal remains the preserved unsupported self-target command.

The formal model gates writes to the stem buffer on less-than-full state and RAA write-buf appends only when buffer-full? is false; SEMSIM and FSMSIM omit a matching named command-token full-buffer rule but provide no contrary full-buffer policy.

RAA and FSMSIM preserve the appended literal bit while clearing the active command source; SEMSIM clears the buffer through its stem special-message wrapper, so AS records SEMSIM as divergent legacy behavior.

additional_source_statuses.summary: guile-asmsim.scm exposes binary write-buf and self-mailbox numeric append behavior, but omits named write-buf-zero/write-buf-one command tokens from its special-message list.
practice/asmsim.scm process-buffer code has id+10b5?/id+11b5? branches and explicit message-code uncertainty, but no named write-buf-zero/write-buf-one command-token surface.
The official PRC TLA files are partial/stub/empty and do not define write-buf-zero or write-buf-one command-token semantics.

as_boundary: Direct self-mailbox and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer execution are implemented through ADR-0161 append behavior and bundled as integrated evidence by ADR-0162; delivered recipient write-buffer command-message inputs remain rejected by the recipient non-init boundary.
